title: Managing Patients Who Want Treatment But Don't Need It | Dr Tim's Tips
filename: Managing Patients Who Want Treatment But Don_t Need It ｜ Dr Tim_s Tips.en.vtt
excerpt: So how do you deal with a client who knows what they want but you don't feel the treatment is right for them [Music] this is a fantastic question it's kind of what a good clinician has to deal with all the time we're always trying to match our treatment to expectations and it sta…

segments.text: so how do you deal with a client who knows what they want but you don't feel the treatment is right for them [Music] this is a fantastic question it's kind of what a good clinician has to deal with all the time we're always trying to match our treatment to expectations and it starts with really understanding what they mean so when a client says they want three males they don't really want three meals what they want is a result and what you need to get back to is exploring the result and it's essentially back to the fundamentals of a good consultation what are they hoping to feel afterwards how are they hoping to look afterwards and what is the treatment that they think will actually deliver them that result because in those three sets of beliefs are all sorts of things that they may get wrong and this is in general practice because exploring the health beliefs you're trying to reach into their mind and understand how they've reached their conclusions and check their thinking because after all they may be an expert in lots of ways compared with you because they're an expert on themselves but probably you're

the expert on what three mills is going to do for them you're probably also the expert on what real beauty is so you are there are various ways that you might need to adjust their beliefs or at least communicate them more thoroughly before just delivering them three meals so i call it the vending machine approach if you just do exactly what your client says and it's a recipe for dissatisfaction for you especially so it's really important to get back to the fundamentals what are they hoping to look like do you think that's really going to work if you're unsure what i suggest you do is you negotiate a different route to that three to those three mills so you would say i'm not sure that's really going to actually make you look good you'd explain exactly why so the ratio of your cheek to your jawline might be too strong that might make your face too wide maybe it doesn't match their the width of their eyes maybe it doesn't match the width of their mouth or all sorts of reasons why you might want to not want to do that so i'm prepared to do maybe one mil see what it looks like and then we can do it together because you well the great

thing with dermal fillers you can see the result you're creating as you go along as long as you've been clear around where the limits are and you've got a good reasoning and your patient accepts those limits because really what you're looking for is a sensible patient who takes your advice and then you can do that in stages what i would say is a contraindication for treatment is if the patient doesn't accept your advice at all if they get into the position where they're saying you just need to do what i want you to do without any negotiation you can't trust that patient and i always say trust works both ways with patients i think a lot of clinicians feel like we need to earn the trust of our patients but actually you need to have trace you need to trust your patient too which means you trust them to work with you to get an a and to achieve a goal that you both are happy with um because it's no good for a clinician if you're not happy with the work you produce and i think it's a fundamental thing you need to get right is make sure you're happy with the work you're producing and that you have a patient who agrees with that you're working together to achieve a common goal if you have different goals you'll never be happy so it basically boils down to a
consultation where you are unified in your goal instead of having different approaches or different goals to the same for the same treatments you
